description: Distributed coordination with TTL for preventing race conditions

evals.prompt: How do I acquire a distributed lock for the pipeline to prevent concurrent executions?
evals.expected_output: Lock acquisition code with proper TTL and retry logic
assertions: Demonstrates checking if lock was acquired

evals.prompt: How do I properly release a distributed lock after work is complete?
evals.expected_output: Lock release code with error handling and best practices
assertions: Shows Lock.release() method in finally block
Includes error handling for release failures
Mentions automatic TTL expiration as fallback

evals.prompt: How does the distributed lock handle retries when lock is already held?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of retry mechanism with exponential backoff
assertions: Provides guidance on setting appropriate retry count

evals.prompt: What are common use cases for distributed locking in this system?
evals.expected_output: List of use cases: pipeline execution, scheduled job coordination, critical section protection

evals.prompt: How should I choose the appropriate TTL for a distributed lock?
evals.expected_output: TTL selection guidance based on operation duration and failure scenarios
assertions: Recommends TTL longer than expected operation time
